observations.recommendation:
Instruct the inventory
Committee to conduct
the physical inventory
of all the properties
and equipment of the
municipality. Also,
require the General
Services and the
Accounting Division
to up-date the property
ledger card to reflect
the true and correct
valuation of the said
property, pursuant
Section 156 of the
COA Circular No. 92-
386 dated October 20,
1992 and Sections 120
and 124 of the
MNGAS for Local
Government Units,
Volume I.

1.5 For the above deficiencies, we reiterated our previous audit
recommendation that the management to
Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all
the properties and equipment of the Municipality. Also, require the General
Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant Section 114, 120 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

2.5 We recommended that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to prepare
the Bank Reconciliation Statement on a monthly basis in accordance with
Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as The
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and such BRS shall be
submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

3.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Reports of Collections
and Deposits for the calendar year 2022 together with the copies of the
official receipt and validated deposit slips to support the total collections
of revenues presented in the financial statements for the end of the year.

4.8 We recommended the Management to:
a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and
every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate
information of supplies on hand;
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance both
in quantity and amount; and
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting Division to
have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock Cards and
Supplies Ledger Cards.

5.7 We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned
Accountable Officers to submit the copies of contracts for the
implementation of the Municipality’s 20% Development Fund on the
structural projects for contract review and inspection, in accordance
with Section 122 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as
the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.1.4 to
3.1.7 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

6.5 We recommended the Municipal General Services Officer to
immediately furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of Acceptance &
Inspection Report within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable
deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU
personnel concerned on other items as required by Section 114 (d) & 116 of
COA No.92-386 date October 12, 1992 and Sec. 5.01.04 of COA Circular No.
95-006 dated May 18, 1995 so that timely inspection of deliveries can be made by
the audit team.

We recommend the management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account as part of Road Networks, pursuant to Sections V.1 and V.2 of the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components.
Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period, in accordance with Sections V.3, V.8 and V.9 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.

We recommend that the Management to ensure that all cancelled checks with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein were submitted to the Commission on Audit for validation and monitoring, in compliance to Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

1. We recommend to the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.reason: Failure to create an inventory committee.

observations.recommendation:
2. We recommend the management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks, pursuant to Sections V.1 and V.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period, in accordance with Sections V.3, V.8 and V.9 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.reason: Failure to create an inventory committee.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommend the management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks, pursuant to Sections V.1 and V.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period, in accordance with Sections V.3, V.8 and V.9 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee will prepare the prescribed procurement monitoring report that would represent all procurement activities for each semester and submit the same to the GPPB pursuant to Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide with Sections 61 and 111 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to submit the breakdown on Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses.

We recommend that the Management to ensure that all cancelled checks with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein were submitted to the Commission on Audit for validation and monitoring, in compliance to Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

3. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely preparation and submission of the Trial Balances, Journal Entry Vouchers and the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB), under the prescribed period for proper monitoring and timely decision making, pursuant to Section 72 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Unit (LGUs), Volume I.

4. We recommend to Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request as provided in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, (2006).

5. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation and to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual audit reports, pursuant to Section 93 of Republic Act (R.A) No. 10964 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year (FY) 2018.

6. We recommend the Management to direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and submit to the Unit Auditor the said monthly reports in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than 5th day of the ensuing month, pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the said Manual.

Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the
properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General
Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to
reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant Section 156
of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992
and Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

Management’s Comment
The management agreed with the Audit Observation Memorandum and assured to
submit and comply the required reports.

We recommend the management to require the Inventory Committee to
conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence
of the balance of the account Road Networks, pursuant to Sections V.1 and V.2
of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23,
2015.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to
facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its
components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and
maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be
prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period, in
accordance with Sections V.3, V.8 and V.9 of the Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

Management’s Comment
I have directed the concerned personnel the General Services Officer and
Municipal Engineer to reconcile the respective records in order to implement this
recommendation in this year.

We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely
preparation and submission of the Trial Balances, Journal Entry Vouchers and
the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the Statement of Appropriation,
Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB), under the prescribed period for proper
monitoring and timely decision making, pursuant to Section 72 of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Unit (LGUs), Volume I.

Management’s Comment
I have directed the municipal accountant to look into this observation. We
acknowledge that there was a delay in the submission of the reports. Our office is
seriously taken action on the said observations and formulated a policy to maintain
a timely submission of reports and other related documents at your office.

We recommend to Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request, in
compliance with the instruction in filling up the Obligation Request as provided in
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.

Management’s comments
The management faithfully adheres to the policy as provided in COA
Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006. I have instructed the concerned
personnel to comply and implement the information needed in the documents such as
the function/program/project (F.P.P.).

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) assign personnel
who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of
Implementation and to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the
ensuing annual audit reports, pursuant to Section 93 of Republic Act (R.A) No.
10964 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year (FY) 2018.

Management’s comments
I have directed concerned personnel of this office to seriously implement and
comply with the submission of the said reports. We are informing your office that this
will make a necessary adjustment and to submit all documents required.

We recommend the Management to direct all Revenue Collectors to
prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated
RAAF and submit to the Unit Auditor the said monthly reports in the
prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than the 5th day of the
ensuing month, pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the said Manual.

Management’s Comment
The municipal treasurer and other concerned employee are now reconciling
their respective records regarding this observation. We will submit the required
reports. Rest assured this office is doing everything in its disposal to implement your
recommendations in this year.

1. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to submit the following documents and reports in accordance with Section 100 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

2. We recommend to the Municipal Government of Ampatuan, Maguindanao to submit the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for CY 2019 in accordance with Section 305 (g) of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit the said recommendation

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) of the Municipal Government of Ampatuan, Maguindanao to instruct the Municipal Development and Planning Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP), Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), Executive Legislative Agenda (ELA) and Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP) in accordance with Section 106 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
5. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

2. We recommended that the Office of the Accounting Office should prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement, effect the necessary adjustment, if any, and submit the same to COA within 15 days pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and 92-125A dated March 4, 1992
observations.reason: This audit observation was reiterated in finding No. 1 of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel
concerned to submit the following documents and reports in accordance with
Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend to the Municipal Government of Ampatuan, Maguindanao to
submit the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for CY 2019 in accordance with
Section 305 (g) of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the
Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) of the Municipal
Government of Ampatuan, Maguindanao to instruct the Municipal
Development and Planning Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the Comprehensive
Land Use Plan (CLUP), Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), Executive
Legislative Agenda (ELA) and Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP) in
accordance with Section 106 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise
known as the Local Government Code of 1991

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel
concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property.
The RPCPPE will serve as the supporting document to the amount reflected as Property, Plant and Equipment in the Financial Position as at end of the year. Any discrepancies between the physical count and the property cards should reconciled and verified and necessary adjustment be made on the latter.

3. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible directs the: Municipal Engineer to submit the accomplishment on the various projects undertaken by the LGU; the Municipal Treasurer to submit complete Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the books of accounts and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within prescribed period by the Commission.

Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the
properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and
the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property.

The RPCPPE will serve as the supporting document to the amount reflected as
Property, Plant and Equipment in the Financial Position as at end of the year. Any
discrepancies between the physical count and the property cards should reconciled
and verified and necessary adjustment be made on the latter.

We recommended that the Office of the Accounting Office should prepare the
Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement, effect the necessary adjustment, if any, and
submit the same to COA within 15 days pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and
92-125A dated March 4, 1992

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for all
governments funds and property, directs the: Municipal Engineer to submit the
accomplishment on the various projects undertaken by the LGU; the Municipal
Treasurer to submit complete Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting
documents (SD) to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording
the same in the books of accounts and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor
within prescribed period by the Commission.

Recommendation:
The Local Executive must see to it that the Accountant and the Treasurer of the agency will regularly submit to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Report together with the bank statements of the Trust Fund.

Recommendation:
The agency is advised to submit regular or monthly accomplishment reports.

4. We recommend that the Local
Chief Executive who is primarily
responsible for all governments
funds and property, directs the
Municipal Treasurer to submit
completely Disbursement Vouchers
(DV) and supporting documents
(SD) to the Municipal Accountant
for proper booking up and/or
recording the same in the books of
accounts and submitted the same to
the Office of the Auditor within
prescribed period by the
Commission.

The Local Executive must see to it that the Accountant and the Treasurer of the agency will regularly submit to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Report together with the bank statements of the General and Trust Funds.

The management is advised to submit physical accomplishment reports for the two Farm to Market Roads projects. The report must contain evidence (picture) clearly showing the actual status of the projects.

Recommendations
1.) Considering the comments of the management and the discussion with the
accountant regarding the issue, the management should recognize or book-up the
Gross Receipt Taxes(GRT) of P2,784.03 and confer with the DBP Cotabato
Branch regarding the interest expenses of 28,123.63 and make necessary
adjustment that might be the result of the reconciliation with bank;
2.) We recommended that the Office of the Accounting Office should prepare the
Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement, effect the necessary adjustment, if any,
and submit the same to COA within 15 days pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-
011 and 92-125A dated March 4, 1992.

Recommendation
1. The municipal accountant should submit the Report of Check Issued together
with disbursement voucher and the ORIGINAL supporting documents within the
period prescribed by Commission. Observe proper guidelines on disbursement in
accordance with PD 1445 and other existing rules and regulations;
2. The municipal treasurer should not release the check for payment unless there
was a corresponding official receipt issued thereof by the payee;
3. The management i.e. concerned officials should notify the auditor within the
prescribed as recited above for the deliveries;
4. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for
all governments funds and property, directs the Municipal Treasurer to submit
completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the
Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the
books of accounts and submitted the same to the Office of the Auditor within
prescribed period by the Commission.

Recommendations
The Municipal accountant must set-up a receivable account for its expected revenue
collection for a given year. The assessment on the real property taxes collections
from the Office of the Municipal Assessor serves as basis of the receivable for a
year. The budget officer should furnish a copy of the budget for the year to the
municipal accountant for setting-up receivable. The following are the suggested
entries:
Setting-Up of Real Property Tax(RPT) Receivable
1. Real Property Tax(RPT) Receivable 1,000.00
Deferred Real Propert Tax Income 1,000.00
2. Collections
Deferred RPT Income 1,000.00
RPT Income(LGU Municipality 40%) 400.00
Due to LGUs( LGUs Province 35%; LGU Barangay 25%)* 600.00
LGU Province(35%) 350
LGU Barangay(25%) 250
Total* 600
Cash 1,000.00
Real Property Tax(RPT) Receivable 1,000.00
3. Remittance to LGUs concened
Due to LGUs( LGUs Province 35%; LGU Barangay 25%) 600.00
Cash 600.00
Setting-Up of SPECIAL EDUCATIONAL TAX(SET) Receivable
1. Special Educational Tax(SET) Receivable 1,000.00
Deferred SET Income 1,000.00
2. Collections
Deferred SET Income 1,000.00
SET Income(LGU Municipality 50%) 500.00
Due to LGUs(only LGUs Province 50%)* 500.00
Cash 1000
Special Educational Tax(SET) Receivable 1000
3. Remittance to LGUs concened
Due to LGUs(only LGUs Province 50%)* 500
Cash 500

Recommendation
The accountant should make a correction/adjustment on the Income Account and
effect necessary adjustment on the respective accounts affected.

Recommendation
1. The municipal accountant should submit the Report of Check Issued together
with disbursement voucher and the ORIGINAL supporting documents,
accomplishment report within the period prescribed by Commission. Observe
proper guidelines on disbursement in accordance with PD 1445 and other
existing rules and regulations;
2. The municipal treasurer should not release the check for payment unless there
was a corresponding official receipt issued thereof by the payee;
3. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for
all governments funds and property, directs the Municipal Treasurer to submit
completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the
Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the
books of accounts and submitted the same to the Office of the Auditor within
prescribed period by the Commission.

NOTA BENE:
SECTION 127. Administrative Disciplinary Action. – Subject to rules and
regulations as may be approved by the President (Prime Minister), any
unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any
requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be
a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer
who, upon being found guilty thereof after hearing, shall be meted out such
penalty as is commensurate with the degree of his guilt in accordance with
the Civil Service Law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the
requirements imposed in this Code shall be conclusive proof that the public
officer concerned is notoriously undesirable [PD 1445].

All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and not income and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Necessary adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.

We recommend that the management will instruct all concerned employees to strictly comply auditing rules and regulations on supporting documents required on government transactions to avoid suspensions of accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for all governments funds and property, directs the Municipal Treasurer to submit completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the books of accounts and submitted the same to the Office of the Auditor within prescribed by the Commission.

All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and not income and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Depreciation should not be recorded as recognized in the case. Necessary adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.
We recommended that the management should make a necessary correction or adjustments for the effect of said transaction, if any.
